dateFormattingLogic.multiDayEvent: Both PHP and TSX: show 'StartDate, StartTime – EndDate, EndTime'

accessibilityVerification.timeElement:
NOT IMPLEMENTED — date/time displayed in plain

tags, no element. Planning spec DEC-008 specified elements with ISO datetime attributes. Minor omission for screen reader date parsing.

issues.description: PHP native date() used for date comparison at lines 46 and 57. Should use wp_date() for WordPress timezone consistency. date() returns server timezone; wp_date() returns site timezone.
issues.recommendation: Replace date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) and date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) with wp_date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) and wp_date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) to use the WordPress site timezone.

issues.description:
Date/time displayed in plain

tags. Planning spec DEC-008 called for elements with ISO format attributes for machine-readable dates/screen reader support.
issues.recommendation: Wrap date display in <time datetime='...'> element. Example: <time datetime='2026-02-20T18:00'><?php echo esc_html($date_time_display); ?></time>. Same fix needed in EventHero.tsx.

issues.description:
Minor TSX/PHP discrepancy: TSX always renders a dateTimeDisplay string (falls back to 'Event Date' placeholder when no startDate). PHP only renders the date-time

if $date_time_display is non-empty. This means the editor preview always shows a date line while the frontend omits it for events with no start date. In practice, TEC always sets a start date, so this won't be visible.
issues.recommendation: Low priority. If alignment is desired, TSX could conditionally render the dateTimeDisplay only when startDate is non-null.

issues.description: The lower group <div class='mt-12 flex flex-col gap-1'> always renders even when all three children (date-time, venue, external link) are absent. This adds 48px top margin with no visible content in edge cases (e.g., test page context). In a real tribe_events context, at minimum the date/time line will be present.
issues.recommendation: Note only — no fix needed. TEC always populates _EventStartDate. If desired, wrap the entire lower group in a conditional check.

observations: All 6 block instances render visually with gradient background (lime/green gradient) — correct Hero CSS applied
Event title 'TEST: Event Hero' renders in large text-header-0 style — correct
Hero section occupies full viewport height with appropriate padding — correct
Gradient fades from top — top-gradient class working
No featured images, categories, or external links visible on test page — expected (requires tribe_events context)
Mobile (375px): title scales down appropriately, layout intact
Desktop (1440px): consistent rendering across all 6 variations

output.testContextNote: This block is designed for tribe_events single post context. The test page (plain WP page) correctly shows the 'TEST: Event Hero' title with no event-specific meta. Full verification of all conditional features (categories, venue, external URL, date formatting, featured image) requires testing on actual tribe_events single event pages. The PHP logic and conditional rendering structure have been verified by code review.
